video_overview: Flipping Physics "AP Physics 1 - Unit 1 Review - Kinematics - Exam Prep" (qP-9wwRrJbg, 1432.76 s / ~23.9 min). Single instructor (mr.p) playing himself plus three student personas (Billy, Bobby, Bo) in a scripted Socratic dialogue over an incrementally-built slide deck. Dense, linear exam-review coverage: (1) exam-prep preliminaries — sig figs policy and a unit-conversion worked example (7-93 s); (2) vectors vs scalars and vector notation (108-190 s); (3) displacement, speed/velocity, acceleration definitions with equations, including instantaneous vs average (190-345 s); (4) UAM/kinematics equations — the 4 equations, 5 variables, equation-sheet notation decoding, sign conventions, the know-3-find-2 rule, free fall, and a calculator sign tip (346-551 s); (5) motion graphs — slope and area rules, then a long worked example building a-t and x-t graphs from a v-t graph and identifying the motion as free fall (551-953 s); (6) a ~65 s Ultimate Review Packet promo (non-content, ~953-1017 s); (7) 2D motion — resolving a vector at an angle with the VERTICAL plus the sine/cosine misconception (1017-1081 s); (8) projectile motion definition, x/y solving procedure, and key facts (1081-1212 s); (9) 1D relative motion — definition, two-car worked example (v_AB = 25 km/hr East), reverse-frame extension, and a sig-figs callback (1212-1401 s); outro to 1432.76 s. Structure.json's unit layer is broadly sound for the first ~20 min but its tail units (u0132-u0138) are semantically shifted about one ASR run-on sentence late (e.g., u0138 labels the outro sentence as equation_introduction; the actual v_AB+v_BE=v_AE equation is spoken ~1290 s), and its 12 practice_prompt units are almost all 1-6 s Socratic checks answered instantly — not standalone practice items — while the true extractable arcs are the 5 worked examples.
inventory:
[1] exam_tip | 7.41-33.52 | Sig figs: the AP Physics 1 exam basically ignores them - use roughly 3 sig figs (core claim 21.44-33.52).
[2] worked_example | 36.18-92.72 | Unit conversion: 8,765 kg/m^3 -> 8.77 g/cm^3; multiply by factors equal to 1 and CUBE the m->cm factor for cubic units.
[3] definition | 108.51-142.08 | Vectors (magnitude+direction) vs scalars (magnitude only) with example lists of each. [SHIPPED in clip 1]
[4] concept | 142.08-189.65 | Vector notation (arrow/subscript/boldface; boldface unlikely on exam) and arrow length proportional to magnitude (15 vs 30 m/s example). [SHIPPED in clip 1]
[5] definition | 189.65-246.44 | Displacement: delta-x = x_f - x_i; zero for round trips regardless of path; distance >= |displacement|; magnitude; 5 m North example; vector. [SHIPPED in clip 1]
[6] definition | 247.60-304.48 | Average speed = distance/time (scalar) vs average velocity = delta-x/delta-t (vector); units; instantaneous vs average velocity. [SHIPPED in clip 1]
[7] definition | 304.48-344.94 | Average acceleration = delta-v/delta-t; vector; m/s^2; instantaneous vs average acceleration. [PARTIAL: clip 1 truncates at 324.28, losing instantaneous acceleration 324.28-344.94]
[8] equation_introduction | 346.06-381.68 | UAM/kinematics equations for constant acceleration: 3 from the equation sheet + 4th helpful one, delta-x = 1/2(v_fx+v_ix)t.
[9] explanation | 381.68-441.84 | The 5 UAM variables and equation-sheet notation decoding: t means delta-t (t_i=0), v_x means final velocity, x - x_0 = displacement.
[10] explanation | 441.84-475.28 | UAM sign conventions (+/- by direction) and the counting rule: 5 variables, 4 equations, know 3 -> find 2. [SHIPPED in clip 2]
[11] concept | 475.28-485.43 | Both UAM velocities are instantaneous (rider on 10/12). [SHIPPED in clip 2]
[12] definition | 485.43-531.44 | Free fall: only gravity acts, a = 9.81 m/s^2 down (use 10 on AP exams), constant a -> UAM applies, v_y = 0 at path top. [SHIPPED in clip 2]
[13] exam_tip | 531.44-549.51 | Square-root calculator tip: be smarter than your calculator about the sign, esp. in the v^2 UAM equation. [SHIPPED in clip 2]
[14] concept | 553.35-620.06 | Graph slopes: x-t slope = velocity, v-t slope = acceleration; delta-x notation collision clarified.
[15] concept | 620.06-678.48 | Graph areas: v-t area = delta-x, a-t area = delta-v; above axis positive, below negative.
[16] worked_example | 678.48-883.04 | Build a-t and x-t graphs from a v-t graph: constant negative a; v-t gives NO initial position (assume 0); equal +/- areas -> zero displacement -> returns to start.
[17] physical_interpretation | 883.04-952.96 | Identify the motion: constant negative a ~ free fall - object thrown up and caught at same height, negligible air resistance; UAM (content ends ~948).
[18] worked_example | 1017.43-1062.62 | Resolve vector A at angle theta with the VERTICAL: A_x = A sin(theta), A_y = A cos(theta).
[19] misconception | 1062.62-1080.53 | "x uses cosine, y uses sine" only when the angle is with the horizontal; correction + walk-through-the-trig-every-time strategy.
[20] definition | 1080.53-1090.26 | Projectile motion: 2D motion near a planet's surface with gravity the only force.
[21] procedure | 1090.26-1157.76 | Projectile approach: split x/y; x: a=0, v constant, v_x = delta-x/delta-t (know 2 of 3); y: free fall, a = -g ~ -10, UAM (know 3 of 5).
[22] strategy | 1161.80-1211.52 | Projectile key facts: delta-t is the only shared variable (scalar); resolve v_i unless horizontal launch (v_iy=0); v_y=0 at top; same-height symmetry t_up=t_down, v_iy=-v_fy.
[23] definition | 1211.52-1241.20 | Relative motion: motion description depends on observer's frame of reference; just vector addition; AP restricts to 1D.
[24] worked_example | 1241.20-1340.72 | Two-car example: v_AB + v_BE = v_AE -> v_AB = 60-35 = 25 km/hr East (what a passenger in B measures for A).
[25] worked_example | 1340.72-1386.48 | Reverse frame: v_BA = -v_AB = 25 km/hr West (car B appears to move backwards from car A). Linked to 24.
[26] exam_tip | 1387.68-1400.78 | Sig figs callback: "shouldn't it be 20 km/hr?" - No, AP exams don't care about sig figs (reinforces item 1).

Unit-layer discrepancies found against the transcript: (a) tail units u0132-u0138 are shifted ~1 run-on sentence late - u0133 labeled "definition of relative motion" actually contains the example question, and u0138 labels the OUTRO sentence (1420.63-1432.76) as equation_introduction with v_AB+v_BE=v_AE, which is really spoken ~1290 s; (b) the sig-figs exam tips (u0003 "claim", tail of u0137) have no exam_tip-like role anywhere; (c) the 12 practice_prompt units are 1-6 s Socratic checks whose answers arrive within a second - none is a standalone practice item, so WAVE-1's practice_prompt anchor priority was chasing non-clips while the real arcs (5 worked examples spanning setup->steps->result across many units, e.g. u0080-u0103 for the graph build) had no single anchor role; (d) the only non-rule dependency signal missed obvious prerequisite chains (UAM equations -> free fall -> projectile y-direction; components example -> projectile "resolve v_i" step). Shipped clip 1 (106.87-324.28) also starts mid-sentence inside the transition (s24, 102.69-108.51) and ends mid-topic.
ideal_clip_count: 20 clips (defensible range 18-22). From the 26 inventory items, merge micro-items: 11 folds into 10; 20 folds into 21; 18+19 ship together; 23+24+25+26 ship as one relative-motion arc (or two, def+example and reverse-frame, giving 21). Breakdown of the 20: 5 definitions (vectors/scalars, displacement, speed/velocity, acceleration, free fall), 6 concept/explanations (vector notation, UAM equations intro, UAM variables/notation, UAM signs+counting rule, graph slopes, graph areas), 4 worked examples (unit conversion, graph building, vector components+misconception, relative velocity incl. reverse frame), 2 exam tips (sig figs, calculator square root), 2 procedure/strategy (projectile approach, projectile key facts), 1 physical interpretation (identify motion from graphs - mergeable into the graph worked example, giving 19). The 2 shipped clips map to: clip 1 (106.87-324.28) = inventory items 3,4,5,6 + half of 7 (an oversized mash of ~5 ideal clips, truncating instantaneous acceleration); clip 2 (441.72-551.06) = items 10,11,12,13 (~3 ideal clips). Net coverage: 8 of 26 items (~31%), 0 of 5 worked examples outside those spans, and entire topics (motion graphs, 2D/projectile motion, relative motion - roughly the back 15 minutes) shipped nothing, confirming the user's complaint.
